cards.title: Android 17 Launches with Gemini AI Features
cards.body: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking tools, parental controls, and security upgrades. The launch includes a Pixel Drop bringing latest AI models to Google devices.

cards.title: Consumers Reject 'AI' in Brand Marketing
cards.body: WordPress VIP survey shows 60% of US consumers find AI in brand messaging off-putting. This conflicts with companies viewing AI search as a key referral channel.

cards.title: SpaceX Goes Public, Valuation Surges
cards.body: SpaceX's IPO pushed valuation to $2.7 trillion, gaining $1 trillion in days. The company now surpasses Amazon in market capitalization.

cards.title: DOJ Backs xAI's Unpermitted Gas Turbines
cards.body: Justice Department claims xAI's gas turbines are critical for national and energy security. Pentagon needs continued use despite permitting violations.

cards.title: Plaud Hits $100M ARR with AI Notetakers
cards.body: Plaud shipped over 2 million AI-powered meeting notetakers while building its software business. The company is establishing itself in the crowded notetaker market.

cards.title: Robinhood Layoffs Skip AI Blame
cards.body: CEO Vlad Tenev announced 10% layoffs without mentioning AI restructuring. Unlike peers, Robinhood avoided the common industry excuse for job cuts.

cards.title: SpaceX Acquires Cursor for $60 Billion
cards.body: SpaceX announced $60 billion stock acquisition of Cursor days after IPO launch. The deal targets strengthening SpaceX's struggling AI division.

cards.title: Probably Raises $9M for Reliable AI
cards.body: Probably secured funding to prevent hallucinations and factual errors in AI outputs. The startup aims for accuracy matching deterministic systems.

cards.title: ChatGPT Market Share Falls Below 50%
cards.body: ChatGPT dropped below 50% market share for the first time with 1.1 billion monthly users. Gemini and Claude continue gaining ground with 662M and 245M users respectively.

cards.title: Respond.io Raises $62.5M, Eyes Deals
cards.body: Malaysia's AI agent startup Respond.io closed $62.5 million funding round. The platform handles customer inquiries via AI agents charged per conversation.

cards.title: Pichai Faces Walkout Over Google AI
cards.body: Google CEO Sundar Pichai experienced protests at Stanford graduation over defense contracts. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about AI use in military applications.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic Cybersecurity AI Models
cards.body: Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull Fable and Mythos cybersecurity models. The government action shows AI industry faces regulatory interference.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Experts Protest Model Ban
cards.body: Dozens of cybersecurity veterans urged White House to reverse Anthropic model restrictions. Experts argue the ban limits ability to defend software and systems.

cards.title: Meta Launches 'AI Mode' on Facebook
cards.body: Meta rolled out new AI features on Facebook drawing from public platform data. The move accelerates Meta's efforts to compete in the AI race.

cards.title: Salesforce Acquires Fin for $3.6B
cards.body: Salesforce purchased AI customer service platform Fin to enhance Agentforce. The deal aims to improve Salesforce's enterprise AI agent platform.

cards.title: Sarvam Becomes India's AI Unicorn
cards.body: Indian startup Sarvam achieved unicorn status with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech. HCLTech invested $150 million in the Bengaluru-based company.

cards.title: NewCore Raises $66M for AI Agent IDs
cards.body: NewCore emerged with $66 million to manage AI agents as enterprise employees. The startup sees AI agent security as the next critical challenge.

cards.title: Satellite Finds Targets Autonomously First
cards.body: An Earth observation satellite successfully located targets independently for the first time in April. The achievement demonstrates AI capabilities in autonomous satellite operations.

cards.title: AI Layoff Wave Sparks Wealth Divide
cards.body: Tens of thousands face job cuts while small group of AI insiders gain extraordinary wealth. The disparity highlights growing inequality in the AI boom.

cards.title: Android 17 Expands Google's Gemini AI Features
cards.body: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking and security tools powered by the latest AI models. The Pixel Drop brings Google's AI capabilities directly to its devices.

cards.title: Consumers Wary of AI in Brand Messaging
cards.body: A WordPress VIP survey found 60% of US consumers find 'AI' branding a turnoff, despite companies viewing AI search as crucial. The disconnect reveals growing skepticism about AI-generated content.

cards.title: Probably Raises $9M for Reliable AI
cards.body: Probably secured funding to prevent AI hallucinations and factual errors in user-facing applications. The startup aims to match deterministic system accuracy levels.

cards.title: SpaceX Acquires Cursor AI Platform for $60B
cards.body: SpaceX plans to bolster its struggling AI division by acquiring the Cursor coding platform days after its IPO. The deal reflects the company's $26 trillion addressable market ambitions in AI.

cards.title: ChatGPT Market Share Dips Below 50%
cards.body: ChatGPT remains the leading AI assistant with 1.1 billion monthly users but lost market dominance for the first time. Gemini and Claude now capture significant user bases globally.

cards.title: Plaud Hits $100M ARR with AI Notetakers
cards.body: Plaud shipped over 2 million AI-powered meeting notetakers while reaching $100M in annual recurring revenue. The startup competes in the crowded AI note-taking market.

cards.title: Malaysia's Respond.io Raises $62.5M for AI Agents
cards.body: Respond.io uses AI agents to handle high-volume customer inquiries with a per-conversation pricing model. The startup plans acquisitions to expand its AI capabilities.

cards.title: Google Faces AI Protest at Stanford Graduation
cards.body: Sundar Pichai was booed and triggered a walkout at Stanford over Google's AI use in defense contracts. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about AI's military applications.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic's Most Powerful AI Models
cards.body: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull its latest cybersecurity AI models citing national security. The ban signals the AI industry faces growing government interference.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Experts Protest Anthropic Model Ban
cards.body: Dozens of cybersecurity professionals urged the White House to lift export-control restrictions on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. They argue the ban hampers defensive AI capabilities.

cards.title: Meta Launches AI Mode on Facebook
cards.body: Meta rolled out new AI features on Facebook pulling from data across its platform ecosystem. The move reflects the company's effort to catch up in the AI race.

cards.title: Salesforce Acquires AI Customer Service Platform
cards.body: Salesforce bought Fin, an AI customer service platform, for $3.6 billion to enhance Agentforce. The acquisition aims to improve enterprise AI agent customization and automation.

cards.title: Sarvam Becomes India's Newest AI Unicorn
cards.body: HCLTech invested $150 million in Indian AI startup Sarvam, bringing the company to unicorn status with $234 million in funding. The deal highlights India's growing AI investment landscape.

cards.title: NewCore Raises $66M for AI Agent Identity
cards.body: NewCore secured funding to develop identity management for enterprise AI agents. The startup argues managing AI agents will become the next critical security frontier.

cards.title: Earth Observation Satellite Finds Things Alone
cards.body: In April, an Earth observation satellite autonomously located its target for the first time ever. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's expanding role in satellite intelligence operations.

cards.title: ALS Patient Becomes Brain Implant Power User
cards.body: Casey Harrell, paralyzed with ALS, has become the first 'power user' of a brain-computer interface enabling him to speak. His three-year experience shows the practical impact of neural AI technology.

cards.title: South Korea's AI Obsession Grows
cards.body: South Korea embraces AI across immigration, commerce, and daily life more aggressively than most nations. The country's cultural embrace positions it as a global AI leader.

cards.title: Pentagon Uses AI to Write Congressional Reports
cards.body: The Pentagon showcased AI's role in automating mandated Congressional reporting processes. The application exemplifies government efficiency gains through AI deployment.

cards.title: OpenAI Losing Billions of Dollars Annually
cards.body: Leaked financial documents reveal OpenAI faces massive annual losses despite dominance in the AI market. The disclosure raises questions about the economics of large language models.

cards.title: Critical Copilot Vulnerability Exposed Users
cards.body: A critical vulnerability in Copilot allowed hackers to steal two-factor authentication codes from users. The flaw highlighted significant security gaps in AI-integrated tools.
